source: https://github.com/ProjectNineveh/panel13 (Models/panel13.obj) -- re-fetched from original public CC-BY-4.0 repo, previous local source was producing the same content
note: Re-exported to fix mesh corruption: prior preview was decimated to 80000 faces under an old target, which fragmented the relief into 271 disconnected components (bratio 0.08). Source re-pulled fresh from ProjectNineveh GitHub repo; raw source is essentially clean (ncomp=3, 2 are 4-face noise specks) when faces are welded by position. Cleaned (dropped components <0.2% of total faces) and left undecimated since source faces (234870) < new 400k target. Result: ncomp=1, bratio=0.006. SECOND PASS: the clean re-export was still single-sided (raw scan only captured the carved face, Z-extent 0.686 vs BM's catalogued depth of 17.78cm against H106.68cm -- about 1/5 the real proportional thickness), so it rendered as an empty void from any angle but straight-on. Solidified by extruding a flat back panel + stitching wall faces around the boundary loop, scaled to match the BM's real H:D ratio (added 2.582 units of depth -> total Z-extent 3.268, matching 17.78cm/106.68cm proportionally). Result: ncomp=1, bratio=0.0 (fully watertight by position-welded check), faces 234862 -> 474004. Not yet checked against the live render pipeline (needs Chrome, unavailable in this session) -- verified instead via offline shaded raster render confirming a continuous solid slab with no inverted-normal artifacts.
